preamble: The raising of nasalized low vowels like [ɑ̃] toward the near-low front [æ̃] is a documented shift in modern Parisian French and other dialects.

examples.note: Shift of the nasal back vowel toward a more fronted and raised position.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
